---
title: "MED-092: Does being on the programme raise word reading via letter-sounds? (period-stacked mediation)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

MED-092 is the **period-stacked** companion to the Phase-0 mediation suite
(#229 recommendation 2). LRP59 (`lrp-rli-med-059`) decomposes the randomised
t1 → t2 intervention effect on word reading (`W`) into the part flowing through
letter-sound knowledge (`L`) and the rest — but it is pinned to the single
randomised window (one row per child, $n \approx 53$). This model refits the
same joint mediator + outcome design over **all three stacked period
transitions** (the gain-factor scaffold), with the exposure being the
per-period **on-intervention** indicator $T$ — the term the gain-factor models
already treat as ignorable — rather than the Phase-0 randomised group.

_The stated, reviewable trade (read before the headline)._ Every quantity here leans on the **gain-factor family's ignorability
assumption** — being on-intervention is exchangeable given the period-start
state, phase, age and the child intercept — instead of pure randomisation. The
on-intervention indicator varies between arms **only in period 1** (after the
waitlist crossover both arms are on the programme), so the exposure contrast is
still anchored on the randomised window; what the stacking buys is that the
mediator → outcome leg ($b_M$, the coefficient carrying the indirect effect)
and every covariate term are informed by **all** periods. Read this model as a
**triangulation companion** to `med-059`/`med-064` — direction and rough-share
agreement is the deliverable — never a replacement.

## Model structure

Both legs are Beta-Binomial on the logit scale over the stacked
child × period rows, each with per-phase intercepts and a per-leg child random
intercept (the gain-factor machinery; the child intercept is a partial,
shrunken stand-in for stable between-child differences — **not** a control for
latent general ability):

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{logit}(L_{\text{post}}) &\sim a_0 + a_{\text{phase}}[p] + a_{\text{trt}}\,T
  + a_L\,\text{logit}(L_{\text{pre}}) + a_A\,\text{age} + \textstyle\sum_c a_c C
  + u^M_{\text{child}} \\
\text{logit}(W_{\text{post}}) &\sim b_0 + b_{\text{phase}}[p] + b_{\text{trt}}\,T
  + b_M\,z(\text{logit}\,L_{\text{post}}) + b_{\text{trt}M}\,T \cdot z
  + b_W\,\text{logit}(W_{\text{pre}}) + b_A\,\text{age} + \textstyle\sum_c b_c C
  + u^Y_{\text{child}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Confounders mirror LRP59's set: expressive (`E`) and receptive (`R`) vocabulary
at the **period start** (the sequential g-formula's history-adjustment rule),
hearing (`hs`) contemporaneous and speech production (`deapp_c`) at the t1
baseline (the gain-factor timing split, A1 decision 2026-07-13). Period-start
baselines at post-crossover transitions are descendants of *earlier* periods'
exposure — admissible for the **per-period** estimand reported here, and
exactly why no cumulative (multi-period) effect is decomposed.

NDE / NIE / Total are computed by counterfactual simulation from the posterior
(the g-formula), averaged over the stacked rows' observed states. Two readouts
are written: `mediation_summary.csv` averages over **all** periods (the
headline per-period decomposition), and `mediation_summary_p1.csv` restricts
the averaging to the **period-1** (randomised, all-untreated-baseline) rows —
the LRP59-comparable readout, mirroring the gain-factor family's period-1
treatment marginal.

**Reading rule:** all of LRP59's non-identification caveats carry over
unchanged — latent general ability confounds the mediator → outcome path,
intervention dose (`IS`) is a treatment-induced mediator–outcome confounder,
and the mediator and outcome are measured contemporaneously within each period
— **plus** the ignorability trade above. These are model-based g-formula
decompositions under stated assumptions, not identified natural effects.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}

## Period-1 restriction (LRP59-comparable readout)

_How to read this table:_ the same posterior and the same g-formula, but the
row averaging is restricted to the period-1 transition — the genuinely
randomised, all-untreated-baseline window — so the decomposition is directly
comparable with LRP59's. Agreement in direction and rough share between this
table, the all-period table above, and LRP59 itself is the triangulation
deliverable.

```{python}
# | echo: false
mediation_summary_p1 = _csv("mediation_summary_p1.csv")
mediation_summary_p1 if mediation_summary_p1 is not None else "No period-1 summary."
```



{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
